title: agathophora postii
side_effects: Agathophora postii can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach cramps, due to its high saponin content. Ingesting large amounts of the plant can also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and difficulty breathing. The plant's toxic compounds can interfere with kidney function, resulting in symptoms like fatigue, dark urine, and swelling in the face and hands. Additionally, improper use of Agathophora postii can also cause headaches, dizziness, and confusion.
